biologicalProcesses.name: positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility
positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility

summary.description: Predicted to enable metalloendopeptidase activity. Predicted to be involved in several processes, including epididymis development; positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility; and positive regulation of sperm capacitation. Located in apical part of cell. Orthologous to human ADAM7 (ADAM metallopeptidase domain 7). [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Jul 2025]

NCBI Canis lupus familiaris Annotation Release 106

biologicalProcesses.name: positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility

summary.description: This gene encodes a member of a disintegrin and metalloprotease (ADAM) family of endoproteases that play important roles in various biological processes including cell signaling, adhesion and migration. This gene is specifically expressed in epididymis where the encoded protein is transferred to the sperm surface during epididymal transit. This gene is located adjacent to a related gene from the ADAM family of proteins on chromosome 14. [provided by RefSeq, Oct 2015]

biologicalProcesses.name: positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility
positive regulation of flagellated sperm motility

summary.description: This gene encodes a member of the ADAMs family of zinc proteases. These transmembrane proteins play roles in multiple processes including cell signaling, adhesion and migration. The encoded protein lacks protease activity and may play roles in protein-protein interactions and cell adhesion processes including sperm-egg fusion. Mutations in this gene may be involved in the progression of melanoma. [provided by RefSeq, Oct 2011]
